title: Open Org Access Grant
description: A record of access granted by an organisation to a requester. The permissions layer — who can see what, when it expires, and a full audit trail.

x-minimum-viable-access-grant.description: The schema requires technical metadata (schema_version, id) which the agent generates. The human-meaningful minimum viable grant adds five things beyond what's auto-generated: who is granting (granter), who is asking (requester), the purpose, the scope of access, and — critically — an expiry. An access grant without expires_at is indefinite access, which the standard rejects. Without granted_by, there is no person-level audit. These together are what makes this a grant rather than a permission. expires_at is enforced as required when status='granted'.

id.description: Unique identifier for this access grant

granter.description: The organisation issuing the grant. Required so the grant can travel through federated channels and the binding to the granting org is explicit (not just implicit from hosting location).

id.description: DID or org-id.guide identifier of the granting organisation

name.description: Display name of the granting organisation

granted_by.description: The human inside the granting organisation who authorised this grant. Required when status='granted'. Provides person-level audit beyond the org-level granter.

name.description: Name of the person who authorised the grant

role.description: Role of the authorising person within the organisation

contact.description: Optional email or identifier for follow-up

id.description: DID or org-id.guide identifier of the requester

contact.description: Contact person or email for this request

decided_at.description: When the organisation granted or denied access

expires_at.description: When this access grant expires. Must be set — no indefinite access.

status.description: Current state of the access grant

purpose.description: Why the requester wants access. Stated by the requester.

scope.description: What the requester can see. Organisations grant access to specific parts of their profile.

profile_sections.description: Which top-level profile sections are accessible. 'full' grants access to everything.

evidence_items.description: Specific evidence item IDs accessible. Empty array = all evidence visible (if evidence is in profile_sections).

strategy_ids.description: Specific strategy IDs accessible. Empty array = all strategies visible (if strategies is in profile_sections).

idea_ids.description: Specific idea IDs accessible. Empty array = all ideas visible (if ideas is in profile_sections).

include_narratives.description: Whether full narrative documents (markdown refs) are accessible, or just summaries

audit.description: Immutable log of what the requester has accessed. Append-only.

detail.description: Additional context, e.g. which evidence item was accessed

notes.description: Internal notes from the organisation about this access grant. Not visible to the requester.
